title: cornus cinerea
intro: Cornus cinerea, also known as the Red-osier Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub native to Eastern Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Cornus cinerea include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and support the immune system. The plant is often prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or infusion for medicinal purposes, and is also used in folk remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
